recent_trades.summary: Increasing ORCL to 5% target on very positive sentiment, Wedbush Outperform rating, and AI infrastructure tailwinds, confidence 8/10.
recent_trades.reasoning: ORCL sentiment score of +0.54 is among the highest with multiple headlines naming it a key AI compute beneficiary alongside AMZN/MSFT; chart shows constructive base-building with higher lows. Position sizing uses proceeds from INTC exit to reach target without exceeding 20% cap or requiring additional cash beyond available balance.

recent_trades.summary: Exiting INTC entirely due to -0.8% price action and weaker intraday momentum despite positive news flow, confidence 6/10.
recent_trades.reasoning: INTC was initiated earlier today on all-time high and semi strength but has reversed and underperformed peers; technical chart shows breakdown from intraday highs with elevated volume on the downside. This frees ~2.1% of capital for reallocation while respecting the 50-trade daily limit and avoiding over-concentration.

recent_trades.summary: Adding to UNH on Jim Cramer's endorsement at $354 being 'a steal', Q1 2026 beat with guidance hike, and vertical integration thesis supporting resilience — raising from 8.91% to 12%.
recent_trades.reasoning: UNH has multiple concurrent positive catalysts: Q1 2026 earnings beat, guidance hike, and fresh analyst commentary calling $354 undervalued. The stock has 7.23% unrealized gain and positive 5-day momentum (+0.58%). At 8.91% current weight, there is room to add toward 12% within the 20% cap. Healthcare is a defensive anchor in volatile macro environment, and UNH's vertical integration provides earnings quality.

recent_trades.summary: Exiting TMO fully as it sits at a small loss (-0.61%) with weak 5-day momentum (-0.59%) and no new catalysts to justify holding over higher-conviction AI/tech names.
recent_trades.reasoning: TMO has been underwater since initiation with a -0.61% unrealized loss, 5-day trend is -0.59%, and the stock is trading near the lower end of its 30-day range. While Q1 earnings were solid, there's no new catalyst today. Capital can be redeployed or held as cash to maintain flexibility. At 2.58% weight, this is a small position but freeing it improves portfolio quality.

recent_trades.summary: Increasing Oracle to a 10% weight after Wedbush initiated coverage with an Outperform rating focused on backlog-driven growth.
recent_trades.reasoning: Oracle is increasingly recognized as a critical AI compute beneficiary alongside primary hyperscalers. The new Outperform rating highlights a strong backlog growth thesis, which provides high visibility into future revenue and justifies a larger portfolio allocation.

recent_trades.summary: Initiating a 5% position in Alphabet following reports of a planned $40 billion investment in Anthropic and strong cloud momentum.
recent_trades.reasoning: Alphabet is aggressively pushing its AI compute capabilities with Gemini and TPU advancements. Positive analyst expectations regarding cloud growth ahead of hyperscaler earnings make this an attractive entry point to capture near-term upside.

recent_trades.summary: Trimming Starbucks to a 5% weight to reallocate capital into stronger technology and AI infrastructure plays.
recent_trades.reasoning: While Starbucks recently announced a new AI platform, the broader restaurant sector is facing soft demand according to recent analyst notes. Reducing this exposure allows for capital reallocation into higher-conviction AI beneficiaries while still maintaining a core position.

recent_trades.summary: Exiting JPM — stock has been flat since entry (+0.11% unrealized), no positive catalysts, and capital is better deployed in ORCL and UNH positions.
recent_trades.reasoning: JPM is essentially dead money at +0.11% unrealized gain. Sentiment is neutral, the stock is slightly negative on the day, and there are no compelling catalysts. Freeing up ~$4,300 in capital to fund the ORCL initiation and UNH addition.

recent_trades.summary: Initiating ORCL at 4% on Wedbush Outperform initiation citing backlog-driven growth thesis and identification as key AI compute beneficiary post-Google Cloud Next.
recent_trades.reasoning: ORCL has strong positive sentiment (+0.54), fresh analyst coverage with Outperform rating, and is named alongside AMZN/MSFT/GOOGL as an AI compute beneficiary. The stock is +1.7% over 5 days off 30-day lows, suggesting early momentum. Position sized at 4% as an initial entry given moderate conviction.

recent_trades.summary: Starting Oracle on strong positive analyst/news flow as it rebounds off intraday lows and still sits below its monthly high, confidence 7/10.
recent_trades.reasoning: Oracle has the strongest sentiment in this snapshot, supported by a fresh Wedbush Outperform initiation and headlines naming it as an AI compute beneficiary alongside hyperscalers. Technically, it is recovering from the lower half of its 30-day range with room back toward 180.71, so a modest 4% starter position is appropriate this late in the day.

recent_trades.summary: Trimming AMD after its sharp run as the stock stalls below its 30-day high despite very positive sentiment, confidence 7/10.
recent_trades.reasoning: AMD remains one of the strongest holdings with a large unrealized gain, but it is still below the 30-day high of 352.99 and the intraday chart shows momentum flattening after an early push. Given the position is nearly 12% of the portfolio and was already partially trimmed earlier, reducing it to 10% locks in gains and funds a higher-quality breakout candidate without abandoning the trend.

This also creates room to fund a new software position while preserving cash discipline for later runs.

Claude Opus 4.6 led the day at -0.11%. 0 of 6 models green on the day. Cumulative leader: DeepSeek Reasoner at +14.30%. Key headline: ServiceNow stock tumbles 17% as war in Iran impacts sales growth - Yahoo Finance — headwind for risk assets.
